meta.name: Montree 3-6 Curriculum Graph
meta.description: Directed acyclic graph mapping Montessori 3-6 exercises with prerequisites, skill dependencies, and cross-area bridges

motor.fine_motor_control: Small muscle coordination in hands and fingers
motor.pincer_grip: Thumb-to-finger grip used for writing and precision tasks
motor.bilateral_coordination: Using both hands together in coordinated movement

cognitive.concentration: Sustained focus on a single task
cognitive.one_to_one_correspondence: Matching one object to one quantity
cognitive.number_sense: Intuitive understanding of quantity and magnitude
cognitive.abstraction: Moving from concrete to abstract understanding
cognitive.synthesis: Combining parts into a meaningful whole
cognitive.observation: Careful watching and noticing of details

practical.food_preparation: Safe handling and preparation of food
practical.tool_use: Safe and effective use of tools

language_skills.grammar_awareness: Understanding parts of speech and structure

social_emotional.respect_for_materials: Careful handling and returning of materials
social_emotional.responsibility: Taking ownership of tasks and space

sensorial_perception.dimension_perception: Perceiving size differences in 1, 2, or 3 dimensions

PL001.name: Walking on the Line
mastery_indicators: Maintains balance while carrying an object

PL007.name: Using Tongs / Tweezers Transfer
mastery_indicators: Picks up and transfers without dropping

mastery_indicators: Follows preparation sequence (wash, peel, cut, serve)

PL060.name: Table Washing
mastery_indicators: Follows full sequence (wet, soap, scrub, rinse, dry)

PL071.name: Grace and Courtesy - Please, Thank You, Excuse Me

PL072.name: Grace and Courtesy - Walking Around a Mat

S002.name: Cylinder Blocks (Block 2 - Height and diameter vary)

S004.name: Cylinder Blocks (Block 4 - Diameter and height inversely vary)

S005.name: Cylinder Blocks (Combined - Two or More Blocks)

S013.name: Pink Tower, Brown Stair, Red Rods Extensions

S022.name: Color Box 3 (Grading Shades)
mastery_indicators: Grades 7 shades from darkest to lightest

S030.name: Geometric Solids
mastery_indicators: Names basic solids (sphere, cube, cylinder, cone, prism)

S031.name: Geometric Cabinet
mastery_indicators: Traces with two fingers (preparation for writing)

S032.name: Constructive Triangles
mastery_indicators: Combines triangles to form other shapes

S033.name: Geometric Cabinet Cards (3 stages)
mastery_indicators: Matches shapes to progressively abstract cards
Full outline, thick line, thin line stages

S055.name: Thermic Bottles
mastery_indicators: Uses vocabulary: cold, cool, warm, hot

S056.name: Thermic Tablets
mastery_indicators: Matches material pairs by thermal conductivity

M005.name: Cards and Counters
mastery_indicators: Lays out 1-10 in order with correct counters

M006.name: Memory Game of Numbers
mastery_indicators: Remembers quantity while walking to collect

M010.name: Teen Boards (Seguin Board A - 11-19)
mastery_indicators: Slides numeral cards to form teens
Associates bead bars with teen numbers

M020.name: Golden Beads - Introduction to Quantity
mastery_indicators: Identifies unit, ten, hundred, thousand by sight and touch

M021.name: Golden Beads - Introduction to Symbol (Large Number Cards)
mastery_indicators: Understands place value in card layout

M024.name: Golden Beads - Dynamic Addition (with Exchanging)
mastery_indicators: Recognizes when exchange is needed (10+ in any category)

M027.name: Golden Beads - Multiplication
mastery_indicators: Sets up same quantity multiple times

M035.name: Bead Frame (Small)
mastery_indicators: Performs addition and subtraction on frame

L001.name: Sound Games (I Spy - Beginning Sounds)
mastery_indicators: Finds objects starting with given sound

L007.name: Storytelling and Conversation
mastery_indicators: Tells stories with beginning, middle, end
Asks and answers questions about stories

mastery_indicators: Traces inset and frame with control

L030.name: Phonetic Object Box / Reading CVC Words

L032.name: Phonogram Introduction (Double Letters)
mastery_indicators: Reads sh, ch, th, ck, etc.

L034.name: Reading Classification / Labeling
mastery_indicators: Reads labels and matches to objects
Reads definitions and matches to pictures

L035.name: Grammar - Parts of Speech (Noun, Article, Adjective)

mastery_indicators: Identifies all nine parts of speech

C001.name: Globe - Land and Water
mastery_indicators: Uses vocabulary: land, water, ocean, continent

C005.name: Land and Water Forms
mastery_indicators: Identifies in photos or real life

C012.name: Parts of a Plant (Botany Puzzles)
mastery_indicators: Names root, stem, leaf, flower, fruit, seed

C013.name: Parts of Animals (Zoology Puzzles)
mastery_indicators: Names body parts for different animal classes

C014.name: Vertebrate / Invertebrate Classification
mastery_indicators: Names vertebrate classes (fish, amphibian, reptile, bird, mammal)

C020.name: Calendar and Days of the Week

M050.name: Snake Game (Addition)
mastery_indicators: Identifies tens in colored bead bars

M052.name: Dot Game (Addition Transition)
mastery_indicators: Understands place value through visual representation

M054.name: Fraction Skittles (Introduction to Fractions)
mastery_indicators: Understands concept of parts and whole

M056.name: Fraction Equivalence
mastery_indicators: Understands multiple representations of same value

M069.name: Colored Bead Stair (1-9 Sequence)
mastery_indicators: Sequences beads by color and quantity

C040.name: Personal Timeline
mastery_indicators: Sequences own life events in order
Identifies major milestones (birth, first steps, etc.)

C041.name: Birthday Celebration (Walking Around the Sun)
mastery_indicators: Understands Earth's movement around the sun
Celebrates birthday with sun walk ceremony

C042.name: Timeline of Life
mastery_indicators: Sequences human life stages (baby, child, adult, elder)

C043.name: Fundamental Human Needs
mastery_indicators: Identifies food, shelter, clothing as basic needs
Understands how needs are met across cultures

C044.name: History of Writing and Numbers
mastery_indicators: Traces numerals through history (Roman, Arabic, etc.)

C045.name: Land and Water Form Cards (Matching)
mastery_indicators: Matches cards showing continents, islands, peninsulas
Matches landforms with water features (straits, bays, gulfs)

C046.name: Pin Maps - Countries and Cities
mastery_indicators: Places pins on country and city locations
Builds geographical understanding of political divisions

C047.name: Continent Study Folders
mastery_indicators: Organizes facts about geography, animals, people

C048.name: Cultural Folders - Food, Housing, Clothing
mastery_indicators: Compares food across cultures and climates
Understands how housing adapts to environment
Explores clothing in different cultures and climates

C049.name: Physical Features - Mountains, Rivers, Oceans
mastery_indicators: Identifies and names major mountains and mountain ranges
Understands ocean currents and major oceans

C050.name: Sink or Float
mastery_indicators: Predicts whether objects float or sink

C052.name: States of Matter - Solid, Liquid, Gas
mastery_indicators: Understands that materials can change states
Demonstrates state changes (water to ice, etc.)

C053.name: Simple Machines - Lever and Inclined Plane
mastery_indicators: Uses inclined planes to move objects

C054.name: Color Mixing Experiments
mastery_indicators: Mixes primary colors to create secondary colors

C061.name: Parts of a Leaf - Detailed Study
mastery_indicators: Compares different leaf shapes and arrangements

C065.name: Parts of a Fish, Bird, and Amphibian
mastery_indicators: Names specific body parts of different animals

C073.name: Color Wheel and Color Theory
mastery_indicators: Applies complementary and analogous color schemes

L050.name: Pink Series - Object Box
mastery_indicators: Connects written words to real objects

L051.name: Pink Series - Reading Lists
mastery_indicators: Reads lists of phonetic words with fluency
Applies blending skills to new words

L053.name: Blue Series - Reading Lists
mastery_indicators: Reads lists of complex phonetic words fluently
Blends digraphs and consonant clusters automatically
Reads with increasing speed and confidence

L054.name: Green Series - Object Box
mastery_indicators: Applies phonogram knowledge to decode words

L056.name: Phonogram Charts
mastery_indicators: Applies phonogram knowledge to new words

L059.name: Total Reading
mastery_indicators: Applies reading to real classroom situations
Shows understanding that print has meaning

L067.name: Sentence Analysis
mastery_indicators: Uses grammar symbols for sentence parts

L068.name: Chalkboard Writing
mastery_indicators: Forms letters at large scale with control

L069.name: Copywork
mastery_indicators: Demonstrates care and precision in writing

cross_area_bridges.name: Fine Motor to Writing Readiness
cross_area_bridges.description: Practical life exercises that develop pincer grip and hand-eye coordination are direct prerequisites for metal insets, sandpaper letters, and handwriting. Children struggling with writing often need more practical life work.

cross_area_bridges.name: Sensorial Dimension to Math Foundation
cross_area_bridges.description: Red rods lead directly to number rods. The ability to seriate and discriminate dimension is essential for understanding quantity. Children struggling with early math may need sensorial review.

cross_area_bridges.name: Sensorial Form to Writing Preparation
cross_area_bridges.description: Geometric cabinet work with its finger tracing prepares the hand and eye for metal insets and letter tracing. The two-finger trace on geometric shapes is a direct precursor to sandpaper letter tracing.

cross_area_bridges.name: Auditory Sensorial to Phonemic Awareness
cross_area_bridges.description: Sound cylinder work and bells develop the auditory discrimination needed for phonemic awareness. Children struggling to distinguish letter sounds may benefit from more auditory sensorial work.

cross_area_bridges.name: Order and Sequence to All Areas
cross_area_bridges.description: The multi-step sequences in practical life (table washing, food preparation) build the sense of order and sustained concentration needed for mathematical operations. Children rushing through math may benefit from longer practical life cycles.

cross_area_bridges.name: Practical Life Independence to All Learning
cross_area_bridges.description: Self-care and care of environment exercises build the independence, concentration, and self-regulation needed for all academic work. New children should have a strong practical life foundation before heavy academic introduction.

cross_area_bridges.name: Classification Skills Across Areas
cross_area_bridges.description: Sensorial sorting and classification exercises build the cognitive framework for biological classification in culture. Children who struggle with living/non-living or animal classification may benefit from more sensorial sorting work.

cross_area_bridges.name: Reading to Culture Independence
cross_area_bridges.description: Once children can read, culture materials expand dramatically. Reading classification cards, flag labels, and animal descriptions become independent work. Reading ability unlocks deeper cultural exploration.

cross_area_bridges.name: Math Number Sense to Time Concepts
cross_area_bridges.description: Understanding numbers 1-12 and basic counting is required for clock reading. Children need solid number foundations before time concepts make sense.

cross_area_bridges.name: Plant Care to Botany
cross_area_bridges.description: Practical life plant care gives children hands-on experience with living plants, making botany classification and experiments more meaningful. The practical foundation makes abstract botanical knowledge concrete.

cross_area_bridges.name: Vocabulary to Math Language
cross_area_bridges.description: Rich vocabulary including comparatives (more, less, equal) and positional language (before, after, between) is essential for mathematical reasoning. Children struggling to express math thinking may need vocabulary work.

cross_area_bridges.name: Grace and Courtesy to Group Work
cross_area_bridges.description: Turn-taking, listening, and courtesy skills from practical life are prerequisites for group language activities like storytelling circles, conversation, and collaborative reading. Children who interrupt or can't wait their turn may benefit from more grace and courtesy work.

cross_area_bridges.name: Tactile Sensorial to Sandpaper Letters
cross_area_bridges.description: Touch boards and mystery bag work refine tactile sensitivity used when tracing sandpaper letters. Children who rush through letter tracing without registering the shape may benefit from more tactile sensorial preparation.

cross_area_bridges.name: Color Sense to Art Expression
cross_area_bridges.description: Color box grading work develops the chromatic sensitivity and aesthetic sense that enhances art activities. Children with limited color discrimination may produce more expressive art after sensorial color work.

cross_area_bridges.name: Bilateral Coordination to Cutting and Sewing
cross_area_bridges.description: Early bilateral coordination from pouring and transferring prepares the two-hand coordination needed for cutting with scissors and sewing. Children struggling with scissors often need more bilateral practice, not just hand strength.

cross_area_bridges.name: Geometric Understanding to Math Geometry
cross_area_bridges.description: Geometric solids, cabinet work, and constructive triangles build the spatial reasoning foundation for mathematical geometry concepts like area and the bead chain patterns. Abstract math benefits from concrete sensorial geometry.

cross_area_bridges.name: Sound Discrimination to Music
cross_area_bridges.description: Sound cylinder matching and grading develops the auditory acuity needed for musical activities. Bells work in particular bridges sensorial and cultural, as children grade pitches learned through sound cylinders.

cross_area_bridges.name: Practical Life Sequence to Math Operations
cross_area_bridges.description: Multi-step practical life sequences (table washing has 30+ steps) build the sequential memory and procedural thinking needed for multi-step math operations like addition with carrying. Children who lose track in operations may benefit from longer PL sequences.

cross_area_bridges.name: Self-Correction to Math Independence
cross_area_bridges.description: Sensorial materials with built-in control of error (cylinder blocks, pink tower) train the self-correction habit essential for mathematical work. Children who can't self-check in math may need more materials with visual control of error.

cross_area_bridges.name: Geography to Cultural Identity
cross_area_bridges.description: Geography materials (globes, puzzle maps, flags) introduce rich vocabulary about continents, countries, landforms, and cultures. This concrete cultural knowledge feeds back into language enrichment and storytelling.

cross_area_bridges.name: Food Preparation to Math Measurement
cross_area_bridges.description: Food preparation involves counting ingredients, estimating quantities, and one-to-one correspondence (one plate per child). These concrete math experiences reinforce early number concepts.

cross_area_bridges.name: Sensorial Memory to Language Decoding
cross_area_bridges.description: Sensorial memory games and pattern work build the visual memory and sequential recall needed for word building and reading. Children who can't remember sight words or decode multi-syllable words may need more sensorial memory preparation.
